section_title: Other Measures: ICOs/ITOs, Unhosted Wallets, Unregistered VDA SPs, AECs, and Mixers

content_blocks.text:
## 17. Other Measures: ICOs/ITOs, Unhosted Wallets, Unregistered VDA SPs, AECs, and Mixers

### 17.1 Approach to ICOs and ITOs

#### 17.1.1 Nature of ICOs/ITOs and Heightened Risks (Paragraph 7.1.1)

In accordance with Paragraph 7.1.1 of the 2026 FIU-IND Guidelines, the Company recognises that Initial Coin Offerings (ICOs) and Initial Token Offerings (ITOs) are generally a means to raise funds for new projects from early backers, and function similarly to Initial Public Offerings (IPOs) for stock offerings.

However, such activities present heightened and complex ML/TF/PF risks, as they typically lack:

(a) Justified economic rationale;

(b) Established and comprehensive mitigation measures;

(c) Full and transparent disclosures.

Persons offering services relating to the issuance, offer, book-building, underwriting, market-making, placement agent activity, sale, distribution, ongoing market circulation, and trading of a VDA would be considered Reporting Entities under the 2026 Guidelines.

#### 17.1.2 Smart Contract Functions and Reporting Entity Responsibility (Paragraph 7.1.2)

The use of an automated process such as a smart contract to carry out Reporting Entity functions does not relieve the parties of responsibility and obligations under Chapter IV of the PMLA and the PML Rules.

In such instances, controlling parties (responsible for the execution of the contracts) qualifying as Reporting Entities shall be required to:

(a) Undertake documented ML/TF/PF risk assessments prior to the launch or use of the platform;

(b) Put in place robust and demonstrable risk mitigation controls.

#### 17.1.3 Discouragement of ICO/ITO Activities (Paragraph 7.1.3)

Given the inherently elevated risks and potential for misuse, ICO/ITO-related activities are strongly discouraged.

In alignment with this regulatory position, the Company shall not facilitate ICO/ITO activities except where:

(a) The Company has conducted and documented a comprehensive ML/TF/PF risk assessment specific to the proposed ICO/ITO;

(b) The Company has implemented and demonstrated robust risk mitigation controls;

(c) The activity has been approved by senior management and the Board (or Board-level sub-committee);

(d) The Company maintains heightened monitoring and reporting in respect of the activity throughout its lifecycle.
